title: Use Mocks, Fakes, and Contracts Intentionally - Quick Check

questions.question: What makes a mock helpful instead of misleading in this course?
questions.answer: It should be narrow, aimed at an owned collaborator, and constrained enough to resemble the real interface rather than accepting anything.

questions.question: What is the rule for patch location?
questions.answer: Patch where the code under test looks the object up, not merely where that object was originally defined.

questions.question: Why might a verified fake beat a deep mock for an external API?
questions.answer: Because it stays fast while preserving more truth about the service behavior your application actually depends on.

questions.question: What is the course's best selection heuristic?

options.B: Pick the cheapest test that still tells the truth about the boundary
options.D: Prefer whichever tool has fewer lines

questions.explanation: The course chooses between test forms by truthfulness and cost, not slogans.

questions.question: When are contract-style checks most useful?

options.A: When independently evolving services need explicit interaction verification

questions.explanation: Contract-style checks are most useful where consumer and provider expectations need explicit protection.
